122120.explanation: Wij Putten strives for diversity, as reflected in our candidate list. The gender ratio in the council is not a strict condition. It’s about a suitable candidate for the position and a good team.

122121.explanation: Wij Putten strongly values citizen participation, which is why we regularly reach out to gather what concerns our residents. Not only around elections but always. For that reason, a referendum is unnecessary for us.

122125.explanation: Wij Putten agrees, but only if there are insufficient other subsidy options from the national or provincial government for those initiatives.

122127.explanation: Companies must feel responsible for housing labor migrants themselves, without it coming at the expense of our own local home seekers.

122131.explanation: Now that the national ban is coming, a show comparable to King’s Day is a good alternative. A show contributes to the connection between our residents.

122134.explanation: Accessibility of public spaces is very important to Wij Putten and the municipality is responsible for this. Accessibility at entrepreneurs' buildings must be handled by the entrepreneurs themselves.

122135.explanation: Wij Putten wants to offer perspective to retiring farmers by allowing more space for small-scale living or other forms of business on their own land. We do this to maintain and strengthen the vitality of the countryside. However, we want to prevent clutter.

122139.explanation: Wij Putten wants to invest extra to raise and maintain indoor and outdoor sports facilities to a higher level. And to support Putten sports facilities as broadly as possible.

122141.explanation: Thirty years ago, we had about thirty officers from the National Police, now only a few neighborhood officers and a handful of special enforcement officers. Both the national government and municipalities must continuously invest in supervision and enforcement.

122142.explanation: Wij Putten wants to deploy camera surveillance specifically in places where there is structural nuisance. At the moment, this could be, for example, in the park de Groene Scheg.

122143.explanation: There should be enough of these kinds of facilities in our country. That could be in Putten, but may also be offered elsewhere in the region.

122147.explanation: People with ties to Putten should be able to live in a recreational home, which can be a temporary solution especially for starters.

122148.explanation: With the current housing shortages, we need locations outside the village boundaries to enable our own young people to live in Putten in the future.

122151.explanation: Wij Putten wants to combat the increasing poverty, and Putten has good arrangements for this. Wij Putten aims to raise awareness of these rules and wants to guide residents through them. We must always keep a close watch to ensure that the arrangements address the most urgent needs.

122154.explanation: This depends on the exact location. For safety reasons, the Engweg is a place where the maximum speed needs to be lowered. Elsewhere, for example the Harderwijkerstraat, 50 km/h is appropriate.

122155.explanation: We must limit the number of care providers to maintain control over quality. For people with specific needs and preferences, the personal budget is always an option. With this amount of money, people can purchase care, assistance, or support themselves.

122156.explanation: Quality comes before price when it comes to our youth. Wij Putten wants to make youth care structurally available at schools, to prevent problems from worsening.

122161.explanation: As far as Wij Putten is concerned, shops may be open on Sunday afternoons. Wij Putten leaves it up to residents to choose how to experience and shape their Sunday.

122163.explanation: Nothing is free when it comes to waste processing. Wij Putten supports the principle of 'the polluter pays'.

122164.explanation: Wij Putten supports the national 'no-unless' principle for the construction of solar parks on agricultural land. The preference is for locations such as roofs and unused areas.

122166.explanation: Wij Putten cherishes intangible heritage such as the dialect.

122168.explanation: At some locations, a residential building of 5 to 6 stories may be built if it fits appropriately in the surroundings.

122169.explanation: The enormous housing shortage calls for unorthodox measures, including room rental. Wij Putten wants to quickly implement a permit system for room rental to enable this while simultaneously preventing nuisance.

122170.explanation: Wij Putten is a strong supporter of free parking and wants to maintain this in the future. Wij Putten wants to make the Kerkplein car-free and create sufficient underground parking behind the town hall.

122171.explanation: Putten is a rural municipality with little public transport where many residents depend on their own car. The new design of the center will remain as it is.

122172.explanation: Wij Putten wants to use existing roads as much as possible for the access to the Halvinkhuizen residential area. In the first phase, the neighborhood must be connected to the Nijkerkerstraat; in phase two, to the Voorthuizerstraat. We are against closing the Voorthuizerstraat at the Welkoop.

122173.explanation: Wij Putten supports speeding up the maintenance of roads and sidewalks in residential areas. This costs extra money but pays off in residential quality.

122174.explanation: Some nuisance due to the redevelopment of the center is part of the entrepreneurial risk. If it becomes no longer reasonable, then compensation from the municipality can come into play.

122120.explanation: The most suitable candidate should become alderman.

122121.explanation: For major decisions, a referendum may be necessary. But residents can also give their opinion through other forms of citizen participation.

122125.explanation: There are already various subsidies for residents to purchase solar panels. Additional subsidies for residents who do this together are not necessary.

122127.explanation: Putten has a large housing shortage. Companies with many labor migrants should not house their temporary workers in homes intended for Putten residents. They should arrange temporary housing themselves.

122131.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality. However, the municipality can grant permits to residents who want to organize this themselves.

122134.explanation: The shopping streets have already been made as accessible as possible for people with disabilities. The shops are the responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves.

122135.explanation: These homes are often large and are sold to outsiders. Subsequently, other farmers are restricted in their business operations. It is better to build new residential neighborhoods like Halvinkhuizen (1,400 homes) with affordable housing.

122139.explanation: The municipality already provides subsidies. If a club is in trouble, the municipality can help. The association must then provide full financial transparency, including payments made to semi-pro teams.

122141.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) should contribute to increased safety, for example in the evenings in the Groene Scheg.

122142.explanation: Cameras at the station and center contribute to greater safety. Cameras also have a preventive effect.

122143.explanation: Putten is probably too small for women to shelter anonymously in a women's refuge. But if possible, GB will certainly support the establishment of a women's shelter.

122147.explanation: A recreational home is for tourists. For a healthy recreation sector, it is necessary that the parks are not inhabited by labor migrants or others who do not belong there. That scares away visitors. It is better to build new residential neighborhoods, even after completion of Halvinkhuizen.

122148.explanation: New residential neighborhoods should be built, starting with Halvinkhuizen (approximately 1,400 homes). Then a next neighborhood with space for at least another 1,000 homes.

122154.explanation: In many places, the maximum speed is already 30 km/h. But on through roads with separated bike paths, such as Voorthuizerstraat, Harderwijkerstraat, and Van Geenstraat, the speed remains 50 km/h.

122155.explanation: People have a choice, but if there are extra costs involved, these should not be paid by the municipality.

122161.explanation: Supermarkets want to open on Sundays. There are no requests from other shopkeepers. Before a decision follows, it is important to hear the opinions of the shopkeepers and also of our residents. For example, via a referendum.

122163.explanation: Nothing is free. Costs are then borne by the taxpayer.

122164.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs, not on meadows.

122166.explanation: There must be attention for the local identity.

122168.explanation: High-rise buildings do not fit the village character.

122169.explanation: Renting out a room is already allowed. But we do not want houses full of labor migrants stuffed into small rooms in residential neighborhoods.

122170.explanation: Parking should remain free with sufficient parking spaces, partly also in a parking garage to be built.

122171.explanation: The city center has become car-free.

122172.explanation: A new southern access road is necessary to safely develop 1,400 homes and to keep through freight traffic out of our village.

122173.explanation: There is backlog in maintenance. This must be caught up step by step. Not everything can happen at once, because then neighborhoods become inaccessible.

122174.explanation: Entrepreneurs can request compensation if they have suffered damages.

122120.explanation: A good reflection of society is important. That everyone gets equal opportunities as well. The Christian Union stands up for diversity. This is not only about being male or female, but also about background or whether you do theoretical or technical work. It's not about exact numbers but about balance.

122121.explanation: The Christian Union finds involvement of residents important. Referendums are not the suitable means for this. Careful consideration and responsibility are important. We warmly encourage consultation and citizen participation.

122125.explanation: Energy coaches visit people’s homes for free. In addition, there are subsidy schemes for the sustainability improvements of homes. The municipality provides residents with information about this.

122127.explanation: It is not the municipality’s task to house seasonal workers. But we do find it important that the housing is humane. And that people do not become homeless if they lose their job.

122131.explanation: On King’s Day this is already a nice event. With the upcoming fireworks ban, this is a good example.

122134.explanation: This is the responsibility of the entrepreneur. The municipality can support with subsidies and advice.

122135.explanation: We are reserved about new construction in rural areas. Building can take place via the 'yes-if' idea, whereby the landscape is strengthened and existing farms are not hindered. Multi-generational living on one farmstead is possible as long as there is no splitting of the property.

122139.explanation: The continuance of a sports club is important. Sport and meeting each other is important. We are happy to support associations, but not at any cost.

122141.explanation: There are already enforcement officers (boa's). The police also deal with safety. However, we find it important to regularly discuss the priorities: more attention for safety or for other matters?

122142.explanation: A safe living environment is very important. In certain places camera surveillance can be very helpful, provided there is a good reason, for example with nuisance in the neighborhood.

122143.explanation: This already exists in the region. However, we find that more attention is needed for violence against women. We prefer to invest in prevention rather than bricks.

122147.explanation: Living in holiday parks is sometimes seen as a solution. In practice, it is often difficult to combine. For entrepreneurs it is financially more attractive to offer holiday rentals than to allow permanent residence, and tourists are an important boost for local businesses.

122148.explanation: In rural areas, we especially want to give farmers space. Housing is only possible with a change of use or for multi-generational living. Therefore, homes are mainly built in Halvinkhuizen (over 1,400). The advantage is that investments can also be made in a school, etc.

122151.explanation: This threshold has already been raised to 130% in recent years. This helps to combat poverty.

122154.explanation: Where it helps traffic safety, we are in favor. Think for example of the Engweg. On the Voorthuizerstraat or Halvinkhuizerweg this is not necessary.

122155.explanation: We find it important that there is room to choose a Christian care provider. At the same time, we are reserved when it comes to Islamic care provision.

122156.explanation: It is primarily about quality and not price. High-quality care, preferably in connection with school, parents and other involved parties, is effective.

122161.explanation: Sunday is an important day for Christians at the start of each week. This has to do, among other things, with the order of creation. A rest day with room for church attendance, social contacts and relaxation. This joint rest day has also proven valuable in our hectic society.

122163.explanation: Much waste can already be disposed of for free, especially recyclable waste.

122164.explanation: No large-scale solar parks on agricultural land, but small-scale, locally supported initiatives such as solar on roofs and agricultural yards.

122166.explanation: Together with the Putten Historical Society, we want to develop an educational package about the history of Putten and about the dialect. The Christian Union would like to hold one municipal council meeting per year in the Putten dialect.

122168.explanation: Putten must retain a village character. Especially in the center, buildings are currently too high. In Halvinkhuizen we do see possibilities to build higher (5-6 floors).

122169.explanation: There is great demand for small homes. Renting out rooms can help young people take the first step onto the housing market.

122170.explanation: There are enough parking spaces. But better distribution is needed: some parking lots are jam-packed while other lots are (half) empty.

122171.explanation: The shopping center is already largely car-free. And the new layout has created more space for walking and terraces.

122172.explanation: A new ring road is not a goal in itself for us, but a means to keep Putten accessible and to relieve the village. The condition is that the road does not attract supra-regional traffic, but primarily connects southern Putten. If there are other means to achieve this goal, we are open to them.

122173.explanation: In recent years much has been invested in new neighborhoods. If it is up to the Christian Union, we will continue to do so in the coming period. But we must not lose sight of the existing neighborhoods either.

122174.explanation: Last year the entire center was renovated. Unfortunately, this caused inconvenience for the entrepreneurs. Therefore, a scheme exists. It is good that the council is in talks with the entrepreneurs who want to be compensated.

122120.explanation: Aldermen are chosen by the municipal council. Quality should be decisive.

122121.explanation: The municipal council must continuously be in dialogue with residents.

122125.explanation: We do believe that with large-scale generation the profit should not go to a commercial developer, but that mainly residents should be able to invest and benefit.

122127.explanation: This is simply the responsibility of the employer.

122131.explanation: No objection to a fireworks show, but this is not a municipal task.

122134.explanation: No, we can stimulate, but it remains the entrepreneur’s own responsibility.

122135.explanation: When stopping farming and demolishing barns, the construction of one additional house is allowed. Preferably, houses are added near the built-up area or in hamlets.

122139.explanation: All associations and institutions that contribute to social goals are eligible for subsidies. This includes sports clubs, but sport is not a goal in itself.

122141.explanation: Safety is primarily the responsibility of the police. This task should not be taken over by enforcement officers (BOAs). When more tasks shift, funding must also come from the national government.

122142.explanation: Camera surveillance can be used incidentally in places with nuisance. Structural use is (still) not necessary.

122143.explanation: Not mandatory, but allowed. Shelter for target groups is a regional matter.

122147.explanation: Recreation and housing conflict with each other. Recreation is very important for Putten and generates 50 million euros annually. We simply have to build. With 1300 homes in Halvinkhuizen, we will get quite far.

122148.explanation: Building on the edge of the village or in hamlets is allowed.

122151.explanation: This limit has already been raised in the recent period to 130% of the social minimum.

122154.explanation: Especially on main roads, this should remain 50. In residential areas, 30 is of course fine.

122155.explanation: This goes without saying. Your faith and culture are part of yourself. Care providers who recognize and understand this promote recovery.

122156.explanation: With the caveat that freedom of choice must remain.

122161.explanation: This goes without saying. For many residents, it is a day of reflection and service to God. Also a day of rest in a hectic society that otherwise rushes on 24/7. In this way, the government can work on broad prosperity.

122163.explanation: It is important that people can easily dispose of environmental waste so it is properly collected and processed.

122164.explanation: No, agricultural land is urgently needed for farmers and food production.

122166.explanation: This is not a municipal task. We do not decide on the content of education. We are positive about it, however.

122168.explanation: We want to preserve the village character. With 1300 homes in Halvinkhuizen as a steady building flow, alongside various smaller projects, we will be well provided for in the coming years.

122169.explanation: This is a serious option but still needs to be researched. Especially where it is possible and where it is not; think for example of parking pressure.

122170.explanation: This may also be underground; for example, in the square behind the town hall.

122171.explanation: This is already largely in practice. With delivery times up to 11:00 AM and for people with disabled parking permits.

122172.explanation: The route has not yet been fixed. Important preconditions are safety, traffic flow, and finances. The most important are maintaining the livability of the countryside and hamlets and full compensation for current landowners.

122173.explanation: Current policy with public space management was established by the municipal council in 2025 at the usual levels. Apart from incidents that must be quickly resolved, it is in order.

122174.explanation: Within the legal frameworks. The municipality must and always does this with plan changes and implementation. However, a threshold of normal business risk and normal social risk applies.

122120.explanation: We are in favor of more women in politics, which is reflected in our candidate list, but that is not a goal in itself. Quality and availability are leading.

122121.explanation: Citizen participation is important. Good governance starts with listening and actively involving residents in decisions. However, we see no added value in a referendum.

122125.explanation: The municipality must support residents, entrepreneurs, and social organizations with knowledge and room for initiatives. Think of neighborhood batteries, energy cooperatives, or green resident projects. This is different from financial support.

122127.explanation: For labor migrants, companies bear responsibility for decent housing. The municipality can—within national regulations—set permit conditions or include requirements in the zoning plan. On-site housing is most ideal.

122131.explanation: It is very unfortunate that this tradition is ending, but we understand this. Once the fireworks ban law is definitively in effect, we will have to determine together whether a fireworks show in Putten is desirable. We see organizing this as a collaboration between various parties.

122134.explanation: Investments in attractive shopping streets already improve accessibility greatly. Shop accessibility is primarily the task of entrepreneurs themselves. We find it positive that the municipality can assist shopkeepers with improvement options, for example a free scan.

122135.explanation: This is already possible to some extent within the rules of change of use.

122139.explanation: We have been advocating subsidies for sports clubs for years. At our initiative, this has been expanded in recent years. However, we can also expect something from the sports clubs themselves. We find this important; ultimately, the municipality is not there to keep sports clubs afloat.

122141.explanation: We want a tough approach to troublemakers and more safety on the streets through enforcers, BOAs, and police.

122142.explanation: We see the importance of camera surveillance but have serious doubts about its practical feasibility and costs.

122143.explanation: The safety of our residents has our full attention, but we cannot provide everything in Putten. If there is a request from the region, we will certainly contribute. We want shelter in the nearby region and to guarantee a direct place in a crisis situation.

122147.explanation: Years ago we invested in strengthening recreation in Putten, and we want to maintain that.

122148.explanation: CDA favors building new homes as much as possible within existing village boundaries.

122151.explanation: In recent years, the limit has already been significantly raised. We believe in the power of society and know that besides financial help from the municipality, there are many other initiatives to assist people. Much is already possible in Putten.

122154.explanation: We agree with most of the built-up area, but there are exceptions to the rule. We think of roads that make a major contribution to traffic flow, such as the Sprielderweg.

122155.explanation: Care organizations must take faith and culture into account as much as possible, but it cannot always be an absolute right. It is important that every caregiver shows respect and is open to the wishes of the care recipient.

122156.explanation: We must also handle healthcare funds sustainably in Putten, but that is never a goal at the expense of care quality.

122161.explanation: This subject has divided Putten for years, as well as shopkeepers and supermarket owners. We are glad that Putten still has a day of rest. In a 24-hour economy, it is good to sometimes pause.

122163.explanation: The current system works well in our opinion. It is fair if you pay (a little) for what you consume. And here too, free never really means free; the costs then end up somewhere else.

122164.explanation: We are absolutely in favor of developing sustainable energy sources, but we prefer not to have them on agricultural land.

122166.explanation: We certainly see 'plat proaten' as part of our local culture as important, but for us it is subordinate to arithmetic and the general Dutch language.

122168.explanation: We want to preserve our village character. Tall urban apartment buildings do not fit here. For us, building up to 6-7 residential floors is acceptable—especially given the current housing shortage—provided it is well integrated into new neighborhoods.

122169.explanation: Yes, the more options for our youth, the better.

122170.explanation: We see—especially during busy times—that there is a high demand for parking spaces. The center plan has brought about some changes. We want to ensure that we do not reduce the number of spaces too much and are open to various parking solutions.

122171.explanation: We think it is a good thing that the core shopping area (Kerkstraat-Dorpstraat) is car-free. We are not in favor of further reducing car access in the center. We see that many people in Putten still do their shopping by car.

122172.explanation: Yes, a southern access route is necessary for better traffic flow and for developments within Putten. Our current road network simply cannot handle more housing.

122173.explanation: In the coming years, Putten will invest heavily in refurbishing roads, sidewalks, and bike paths—and that is urgently needed. Extra funding has already been allocated for this.

122174.explanation: There are already schemes available that retailers can tap into for compensation.

122120.explanation: That would be good for the balance. Until now, only once has a college member been a woman, namely Alette ter Borg-Guliker from the VVD.

122121.explanation: Through a Citizens’ Initiative and a Citizen’s Council, there are more opportunities for citizens to exert direct influence. It may be good to choose topics carefully when they are fundamental for our beautiful village. Think, for example, about whether or not to have an asylum seekers’ center or shops open on Sundays.

122125.explanation: Together with residents we make sustainability plans per neighborhood. Without higher housing costs. We build smart networks instead of unprofitable heating networks. We look, for example, at heat storage under artificial grass fields, which can heat 200 homes.

122127.explanation: Residential neighborhoods on industrial estates are undesirable and lack transparency.

122131.explanation: That would be fine, even great.

122134.explanation: People with disabilities may well receive a little support for accessibility in various buildings. If we can help financially towards entrepreneurs in this, that is fine. Our town hall could use a lift to the top floor and an accessible public toilet.

122135.explanation: Farmers deserve space; for innovation, entrepreneurship, and future-proof agriculture. More opportunities on the farmyard: recreation, tourism, or nature management are allowed, as long as the landscape is preserved. Building houses can be allowed, but only in a limited way.

122139.explanation: Equal treatment of sports clubs: the own contribution of 15% for outdoor sports investments should be abolished, because there is no own contribution for indoor sports. Fair is fair. Strong clubs: structural support and long-term subsidies for maintenance.

122141.explanation: More visibility on the streets: additional enforcement officers and forest rangers in the outskirts. Enforcement must be tangible. We also need to prepare for emergencies. Increasing the number of enforcement officers is necessary for our current and future safety.

122142.explanation: Smart camera surveillance: deploy where it works. We make maximum use of our powers. We want zero tolerance for intimidation or violence.

122143.explanation: If there is a need for this and it is supported by care organizations, then we are definitely in favor.

122147.explanation: Active and fair approach concerning recreational homes: enable transformation to residential use where appropriate, with consent of residents and owners.

122148.explanation: Let's simply focus in Putten on building homes for our own villagers. We have been talking for 10 years or more about, for example, Halvinkhuizen. In the past 4 years, not a single house has been built there.

122151.explanation: Many social schemes are national and have their own criteria. It is better to provide customized solutions. We must further focus on that.

122154.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount, but traffic flow in Putten is also important. Where 30 km/h is most responsible, we should set that speed. This is already happening.

122155.explanation: Fixed family professional: one helper per family for trust and effectiveness. Care is leading here, not religion, culture, or beliefs.

122156.explanation: As VVD Putten, we find quality the most important. This benefits our villagers.

122161.explanation: Meanwhile, we see that supermarkets and shops in neighboring municipalities are open, leading to loss of turnover for our own entrepreneurs. As far as VVD is concerned, supermarkets in Putten should be allowed to open on Sundays as well. We leave it up to the entrepreneur, staff, and you as a customer.

122163.explanation: We have a few places in Putten where waste can be disposed of.

122164.explanation: We use the energy transition as an opportunity to stimulate innovation and employment in the region. We want faster procedures for solar panels. First, we look at solar panels on roofs, but farmers deserve space for innovation and entrepreneurship on their own land.

122166.explanation: Education as a priority: less consultation, more results. We choose decisiveness, clarity, and quality. We already see education under pressure. Addressing language and learning backlogs and just learning arithmetic and reading is our priority.

122168.explanation: We must honor our Putten character in building, but an extra residential floor in the village center is acceptable. For 4 years we have advocated for apartment complexes at Strand Nulde, Putten at Sea. Beautiful starter and senior apartments with a wonderful view.

122169.explanation: As a pilot project, we can be open to this. There is an enormous shortage of housing, and if this is a possible solution then a trial should be possible.

122170.explanation: We advocate for underground parking. This can be done, for example, at Brinkstraat. Then we can also make it suitable as a shelter in case of an emergency.

122171.explanation: The center has just been refurbished and has become less car-friendly. There is now a nice balance. You can go through the center on foot, by bike, and where necessary, by car.

122172.explanation: A full bypass to the A28 to keep truck traffic out of the center. This means, besides the southern bypass (there is now a preliminary proposal for this), also a connection to the A28. To avoid long queues in and out of town, a real bypass is necessary.

122173.explanation: Certainly. In some neighborhoods it was unfortunately decided to proceed at a low level due to accumulated backlogs. For VVD Putten, this is incomprehensible!

122174.explanation: By starting late and also delivering things later than planned, it is very justifiable to give our entrepreneurs a helping hand for all lost turnover and profit. On average, 10% fewer customers could visit during this period.

122120.explanation: Preference for equal representation, with attention also to aspects other than male/female.

122121.explanation: The opportunities for residents to participate in decision-making are significant, but are not yet sufficiently utilized and are insufficiently known to many residents.

122125.explanation: Energy cooperatives help prevent grid congestion and foster a sense of community.

122131.explanation: With the fireworks ban in place, it is important to investigate the needs. If a fireworks show is organized by the municipality, we must explore how the municipality can facilitate this.

122134.explanation: Under conditions of quality and accountability.

122135.explanation: Only in exceptional cases based on established regulations.

122139.explanation: Sports are important for health and social cohesion. Set requirements for the spending of funds and their accountability.

122141.explanation: Many measures cannot be properly enforced due to a shortage of enforcement officers.

122142.explanation: More enforcement officers for law enforcement are much more effective. Cameras raise privacy concerns.

122143.explanation: Investigate the extent of the need and respond together with neighboring municipalities. Putten must contribute its fair share.

122147.explanation: Only by exception based on criteria established by the current council.

122148.explanation: The landscape in the rural area deserves protection, not clutter from small clusters of new construction. Adjusting facilities accordingly is very expensive.

122151.explanation: This measure is socially important and addresses needs within the municipality.

122154.explanation: This is already the case in large parts of the village. Make 30 km/h the standard and examine where 50 km/h is desired or necessary. Adapt the street image so it is clear.

122155.explanation: Where possible, but given the small scale of our village, not always feasible.

122156.explanation: Besides price, quality and proximity (local or regional organization) are important.

122161.explanation: Supermarkets and other stores in Putten currently lose turnover due to open stores in neighboring municipalities.

122163.explanation: This promotes proper waste disposal and prevents illegal dumping.

122164.explanation: Solar panels primarily on existing roofs (municipal buildings, homes, businesses) and only exceptionally on land less important for agriculture and nature.

122166.explanation: Primary schools have the options for this; it is up to them to make that choice together with the parents.

122168.explanation: Residential towers do not fit the village character. Two or, in exceptional cases, three residential floors are sufficient.

122169.explanation: Only by exception and based on rules to be established.

122170.explanation: The current number of parking spaces is sufficient. However, more disabled parking spaces and bicycle parking are needed.

122171.explanation: Further research must be done here with the premise that access for slow traffic is maintained.

122172.explanation: Access to Halvinkhuizen can be via the already planned access to the Henslare roundabout. Further consultation with all involved is needed regarding the utility and necessity of a bypass. The current preferred route is insufficiently thought through.

122173.explanation: Preventive inspections can identify potential problems and malfunctions. Quick actions after reports of poor maintenance.

122174.explanation: The renovation was announced on time, carried out at a high speed, and after the renovation the shopping area became more attractive.

statements.title: Putten should spend more money on the maintenance of pavements and streets.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: More high-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Putten.
Putten needs more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Putten should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The residents of Putten should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Putten moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Putten should have a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Putten should build a bypass on the south side of the village.
The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include the Putten dialect in their teaching.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
Putten's town centre should become car-free.
Putten should compensate businesses for the disruption caused by the renovation of the town centre.
